hero.title: 5 Days, Bangalore to the Nilgiris.
hero.titleAccent: Palaces, Gardens and the Famous Toy Train.
hero.intro: From easygoing Bangalore to the royal splendour of Mysore and up into the cool blue Nilgiri hills of Ooty, with a ride on the famous mountain toy train. A classic South India loop through cities, palaces and hill stations.

breadcrumb.label: 5 Days Bangalore, Mysore & Ooty Scenic Toy Train

hero.heroImageAlt: Mysore Palace and Nilgiri toy train Ooty

overview.heading: 5 Days Bangalore, Mysore & Ooty Scenic Toy Train: The Full Picture
body: This 5 day loop is one of the most enjoyable in South India, linking the garden city of Bangalore, the palace city of Mysore, and the colonial hill station of Ooty in the Nilgiris. The scenery shifts from urban to royal to misty mountains, with the famous Nilgiri toy train as a highlight.

Arranged privately from Malaysia, the trip is relaxed and varied, with your own vehicle and driver guide. You see the grand Mysore Palace lit up, ride the UNESCO-listed mountain railway, and enjoy the cool air and tea gardens of Ooty, all in a comfortable five days.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Mysore Palace
items.body: The dazzling Amba Vilas Palace, one of the grandest royal residences in India, especially magical when illuminated by nearly 100,000 bulbs on certain evenings.

items.title: The Nilgiri Toy Train
items.body: The UNESCO-listed mountain railway climbs through forests and tea gardens into the Nilgiri hills, a slow, scenic ride that is a highlight of any South India trip.

items.title: Ooty's Blue Hills
items.body: Cool air, tea gardens, lakes and colonial charm in the queen of South Indian hill stations, a refreshing change from the plains.

days.title: Arrive Bangalore
days.description: Met at Bangalore airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening at leisure.

days.title: Bangalore to Mysore
days.description: Bangalore highlights, then drive to Mysore.

days.title: Mysore to Ooty
days.description: Mysore sights, then a scenic drive up to Ooty.

days.title: Ooty & Toy Train
days.description: Ooty sightseeing and a ride on the Nilgiri toy train.

days.title: Return & Depart
days.description: Drive back towards Bangalore or Coimbatore for your onward flight.

whyBook.heading: South India's Classic Loop, Done Right
whyBook.body: Linking Bangalore, Mysore and Ooty with the toy train takes good timing and routing. We sequence it so the scenery flows and the train slots in. Dhesu has run South India tours since 1988.

points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing

sections.heading: Bangalore, Mysore & Ooty Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 5 day loop is one of the most rewarding short tours in South India, combining the garden city of Bangalore, the royal palace city of Mysore, and the cool Nilgiri hill station of Ooty.

The famous Nilgiri toy train, a UNESCO World Heritage railway, is a highlight, climbing through forests and tea gardens. A private vehicle links it all at a relaxed pace.

It is an ideal trip for travellers who want variety: cities, palaces and hill stations in one journey.

sections.heading: The Nilgiri Mountain Railway
sections.body:
The Nilgiri Mountain Railway is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, a charming narrow-gauge line that climbs steeply into the hills around Ooty. The slow, scenic journey through tunnels, forests and tea estates is an experience in itself.

We include a ride on this iconic toy train, one of the most memorable parts of any Bangalore, Mysore and Ooty tour.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Bangalore or Coimbatore Airport (BLR / CJB). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: Is the toy train ride included?
items.answer: Yes, the package includes a ride on the famous Nilgiri toy train. Seats can be in demand, so we book ahead where possible. We will confirm the segment and timing as part of your itinerary.

items.question: What is the best time for this trip?
items.answer: October to March offers pleasant weather across all three destinations. Ooty is cool year-round given its elevation, a refreshing contrast to the warmer plains. The monsoon brings lush scenery but can affect hill roads and train schedules.

items.question: Is Ooty cold?
items.answer: Ooty is cool and pleasant thanks to its high elevation in the Nilgiris, especially in the mornings and evenings. A light jacket is useful. It is a refreshing change from the warmer cities and a highlight of the loop.
